title: UUID Generator
description: Free online UUID v4 generator. Instantly create one or multiple unique identifiers. No libraries required — generated entirely in your browser using the Web Crypto API.

howToUse.label: Set count
howToUse.description: Choose how many UUIDs to generate (1–100).

howToUse.label: Configure format
howToUse.description: Toggle uppercase and hyphen options to match your needs.

howToUse.label: Generate
howToUse.description: Click "Generate" to create fresh UUID v4 values.

howToUse.label: Copy
howToUse.description: Click any UUID to copy it, or use "Copy All" to copy the full list.

faq.question: What is a UUID v4?
faq.answer: UUID v4 (Universally Unique Identifier version 4) is a 128-bit random identifier formatted as 32 hexadecimal digits separated by hyphens: xxxxxxxx-xxxx-4xxx-yxxx-xxxxxxxxxxxx. Version 4 means the values are randomly generated.

faq.question: Are these UUIDs truly unique?
faq.answer: UUID v4 uses 122 random bits, giving approximately 5.3 × 10^36 possible values. The probability of a collision is astronomically small and negligible for practical purposes.

faq.question: Is this tool safe to use?
faq.answer: Yes. UUIDs are generated using the browser's built-in crypto.randomUUID() API (Web Crypto API). No data is sent to any server — everything happens locally in your browser.

faq.question: What's the difference between UUID and GUID?
faq.answer: GUID (Globally Unique Identifier) is Microsoft's term for the same concept. They are structurally identical and interchangeable in most contexts.

faq.question: When should I use a UUID?
faq.answer: UUIDs are ideal as primary keys in databases, session tokens, correlation IDs for logging, file names for uploaded assets, and any situation where you need a collision-resistant unique identifier without a central coordinator.
